parsed_review.summary: CP00-419 is a descriptor-only payments-core pack covering RP13.P09.M06.S12-RP13.P09.M07.S01 (9 Synthetic Fixture Set rows + 1 Test And Golden Case Set row; 8 implementation + 2 claude_review = 10 units). Pack binding, deliverable/micro distributions, and the next-pack handoff (CP00-420 / RP13.P09.M07.S02) are consistent across manifest.json, review-baseline.json, closeout-pack-plan.json, registry.js (PAYMENTS_CORE_CP419_PACK_BINDING), and payments-core-contract.json. The createPaymentsCoreCp419* functions build frozen descriptor objects with no runtime dispatch, no DB/object-storage I/O, no audit/state writes, and no real-data loading; validators.js, model.test.js, and validate-rp13-payments-core-contract.mjs assert descriptor_only/runtime_execution=false on every row plus the no-write attestation (promotes_claude_to_final_approval=false, claims_enterprise_trust_from_local_validation=false). Authority boundary is correct: claude_is_final_approval=false and human_final_approval_required_for_runtime_opening=true; Hermes/Claude packets are deterministic review packet/receipt descriptors (H13/C13), not product runtime. production_ready is correctly false with status implemented_pending_review, so no enterprise-trust claim is asserted from local validation. Review-hardening evidence is valid: readiness auth-preflight passed with well-formed JSON, prompt is 2152 bytes (< 25600) with no embedded full diff/source, and tools are restricted to Read/Grep/Glob; there are no prior invalid review attempts to reject. Two read-only-review caveats, neither a defect: (1) command-evidence.json outcomes (npm test/build/validate, git diff --check) are self-reported and were not re-executed under this read-only review; (2) the post-review artifacts claude-review-result.json, adjudication.md, and construction-inspection.json listed in evidence_refs are expected to be generated in later hardening steps (finding_adjudication, construction_inspection) and are correctly absent at the review step. One P3 metadata inconsistency in changed-file-scope.json is noted below; it is non-blocking and does not affect the descriptor-only boundaries or the production_ready posture. No P0/P1/P2 issues found; the pack-level claim (descriptor verified, production_ready=false) is supported.

findings.title: changed-file-scope.json double-classifies the pack artifact directory and handoff doc as both implementation and unrelated
findings.details: docs/closeout-packs/cp00-419/ and docs/closeout-pack-plan/new-session-handoff.md appear in BOTH untracked_implementation_files (lines 131-132) and unrelated_dirty_files_preserved (lines 137-138). The pack's own artifact directory cannot logically be 'unrelated' to the pack. The authoritative manifest.json worktree_transition_policy.unrelated_dirty_files_preserved (lines 544-552) correctly excludes both, and active_untracked_implementation_files_omitted is [], so no in-scope implementation file was hidden from review. Impact is confined to reviewer-aid metadata accuracy and does not affect any runtime/no-write boundary or the production_ready posture.
findings.recommendation: In the changed-file-scope generator, remove docs/closeout-packs/cp00-419/ (and the pack handoff doc, if treated as pack-scoped) from unrelated_dirty_files_preserved so the classification matches manifest.json. Non-blocking; may be corrected in a later generation pass.
findings.post_review_disposition: Non-blocking metadata nit. Does not block pack or goal closeout; descriptor-only boundaries, no-write attestation, and authority boundary are unaffected.
